stops.name: Parque Las Palapas
stops.shortDescription: The main square and true local hub of downtown Cancun. In the evenings, it comes alive with food carts selling marquesitas, live music, and families enjoying the open-air plaza.

stops.name: Catedral de la Santísima Trinidad
stops.shortDescription: A modern, modest Catholic church situated right near the main park. It reflects the newer, growing population of downtown Cancun compared to Mexico's colonial-era cathedrals.

stops.name: Mercado 28
stops.shortDescription: A massive, maze-like flea market spanning an entire city block. It is the best place in the city to practice your haggling skills for authentic Mexican pottery, silver jewelry, and souvenirs.

stops.name: Plaza Bonita
stops.shortDescription: Built to resemble a traditional Mexican colonial village, this charming shopping plaza sits adjacent to Mercado 28. It offers a quieter, more relaxed atmosphere with artisan shops and cafes.

stops.name: Monumento a la Historia
stops.shortDescription: Located on a bustling roundabout on Avenida Tulum, this distinct monument pays tribute to Mexican history. Its bold, modern design celebrates the nation's indigenous and revolutionary roots.

stops.name: Palacio Municipal de Cancún
stops.shortDescription: The city hall and administrative center for the municipality of Benito Juárez. The large plaza in front often hosts civic events, local protests, and cultural exhibitions.

stops.name: Teatro de la Ciudad Cancún
stops.shortDescription: The premier venue for performing arts in downtown Cancun, frequently hosting local theater productions, folkloric dance performances, and community cultural festivals.

stops.name: Iglesia de Cristo Rey
stops.shortDescription: Recognized as the very first Catholic church built in Cancun. It holds a special historical significance for the pioneers who founded the city in the early 1970s.

stops.name: Luum Pakul Artisan Market
stops.shortDescription: A small, vibrant cooperative market where local artisans sell beautiful handcrafted hammocks, textiles, and traditional Mayan souvenirs away from the major crowds.

stops.name: Avenida Yaxchilán
stops.shortDescription: A bustling downtown street famous for its traditional Mexican cantinas, massive authentic taquerias, and roaming mariachi bands that entertain guests late into the night.

stops.name: Glorieta del Ceviche
stops.shortDescription: A famous traffic circle on Avenida Tulum featuring a massive monument shaped like conch shells and starfish. It is the central gathering place for city-wide celebrations and sports victories.

stops.name: Plaza de Toros
stops.shortDescription: Cancun's historic bullring. While bullfighting is no longer the main attraction, the outer perimeter of the arena is now a thriving hotspot packed with popular local bars and taco stands.

stops.name: Malecon Tajamar
stops.shortDescription: A beautifully paved, wide boardwalk offering stunning, peaceful views across the Nichupté Lagoon. It is a favorite local spot for jogging, biking, and watching the sunset.
